x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces CCRB's open accountability data (complaints, allegations, accused officers, penalties) and adds file_misconduct_complaint — the net-new write workflow that today lives only in the online web form or a 311 call.

server.name: io.nyc.ccrb
server.title: NYC Civilian Complaint Review Board (CCRB)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Civilian Complaint Review Board — closed misconduct complaints, per-allegation FADO records, accused-officer complaint histories, and discipline/penalty outcomes from CCRB's Data Transparency Initiative and NYC Open Data, plus the net-new ability to file a police-misconduct complaint against an officer, which today lives only in the online web form.
server.instructions: Use find_complaints / get_complaint (and get_complaint_allegations) to search closed NYPD-misconduct complaints by borough, precinct, disposition, or date and drill into their allegations. find_allegations searches the finest-grain FADO records (Force, Abuse of Authority, Discourtesy, Offensive Language) with CCRB/NYPD dispositions. find_officers / get_officer return accused-officer complaint histories keyed on Tax ID; find_penalties returns discipline outcomes for substantiated cases (APU + NYPD). file_misconduct_complaint (net-new) files a new complaint against an officer; it mutates state — confirm the incident description, FADO category, location, and any officer identification with the user before submitting, and note that complaints may be filed anonymously but CCRB cannot follow up without contact information. check_complaint_status reads a filed complaint's status by tracking number.

tools.name: find_complaints
tools.title: Find Complaints
tools.description: Search closed complaints of NYPD misconduct by borough, precinct, CCRB disposition, received-date range, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_complaint
tools.title: Get Complaint
tools.description: Fetch a single misconduct complaint (incident, disposition, evidence flags, geography) by Complaint Id.

tools.name: get_complaint_allegations
tools.title: Get Complaint Allegations
tools.description: List the individual FADO allegations recorded within a complaint.

tools.name: find_allegations
tools.title: Find Allegations
tools.description: Search allegations against officers by FADO type, specific allegation, CCRB disposition, officer Tax ID, or complaint.

tools.name: find_officers
tools.title: Find Officers
tools.description: Search accused NYPD officers and their complaint counts by command, rank, minimum total complaints, minimum substantiated complaints, or free text.

tools.name: get_officer
tools.title: Get Officer
tools.description: Fetch an accused officer's complaint profile (rank, command, total and substantiated complaints) by NYPD Tax ID.

tools.name: find_penalties
tools.title: Find Penalties
tools.description: Search discipline / penalty outcomes for substantiated complaints (APU trial track and NYPD final penalty) by officer Tax ID, complaint, APU status, or APU flag.

tools.name: file_misconduct_complaint
tools.title: File a Misconduct Complaint
tools.description: File a complaint of NYPD misconduct against an officer (report an incident). NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — it lives only in the online web form, a 311 call, mail, or in person. Mutates state — confirm the incident description, FADO category, location, and any officer identification first; complaints may be filed anonymously but CCRB cannot follow up without contact information.

tools.name: check_complaint_status
tools.title: Check Complaint Status
tools.description: Read the processing status of a filed complaint by its tracking number — today checked only through the Complaint Status Lookup app.
